title: carum appuanum subsp. palmatum
intro: Carum appuanum subsp. palmatum, also known as mountain caraway or carum palmatum, is a plant species in the carrot family that is native to the mountainous regions of Italy. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, as it is known to have carminative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Carum appuanum subsp. palmatum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea, making it a valuable herb for promoting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Carum appuanum subsp. palmatum can be made by infusing the dried leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, or by using the seeds as an ingredient in medicinal tinctures and salves.
